pages.text: Amir loves Grandma Rabiya’s kitchen. He loves the spices… the smells… and the sizzling sounds. Amir wants to learn to fold Grandma’s famous samoosas.
! o N square? laughs Amir. Are samoosas
Are they circles? n y, a n r G o, N g l e s! a n r i t e r ’ y e h t

They sing Great Great Grandma’s Great- - - samoosa song. p o t a t o e s h e t o p h C chop chop! chop,, h e s p i c e s n t e i a k h S shake, shake! shake, a m o o s a s e s t h d o l F fold, fold! fold,
Amir copies Grandma. Fold the dough once… Fold the dough twice…
Then fill it and fold it and paste it and But Amir’s samoosas look… done!
…different. e n o t t r i a y ’ r n g e l e h s T! I don’t know what shape they are. But they’re not the same! I give up!
Things don’t have to be the same, Amir. It’s the ddiiffffeerreennccee that makes the difference! Grandpa Farouk walks in and picks up a special-shaped samoosa …
C H! N U R C Hmmm, now that’s a tasty samoooooosa!
You see! Your special-shaped samoosas are a hit. H i g h fi v e!
From that day on Amir would chop chop! chop,, shake, shake! shake, fold, fold! fold, Just like Great-Great-Great-Grandma.
e t n r e! e s wr u e o ff s m ai d s a oo f t i ll mb i t s e a l t s tt u s b i l i Ha
How many shapes did you find?
